metadata.title: RNA-Protein Interaction Classification via Sequence Embeddings
organizations: University of Freiburg, Germany; Konrad Zuse School of Excellence; ELLIS Institute Tübingen

sections.title: In a nutshell
content: RNA and proteins form complexes throughbinding interactions, crucial foradvances in biology or medicine, such as drug development.
Predicting RNA-protein interactions (RPIs) computationally is challenging due todata scarcityand itsbias.
We presentRNAInterAct, the largest publicly available dataset withbiologically plausible negative interactions.
The dataset includessequence informationand precomputedembeddings that capture meaningful informationabout interactors' structure and function.
A meticulous train/test split based on theRNA familyenables evaluation on truly unseen interactions.
We introduceRPIembeddor, a transformer-based model for binary interaction classification and demonstrate its strong performance.

figures.description: RPIembeddor model architecture diagram showing encoder layers and interaction probabilities
Detailed view of the Multi-Head Attention block within the transformer

sections.title: Ablation studies
content: Both embeddings contribute to RPIembeddor's performance.

sections.title: RNAInterAct
content: Compilation of the RNAInterAct dataset in five steps:
2. Cross-linking large scale databases toobtain sequence information.
4.Annotatingproteins with clan and RNA with RNA family information.
The final dataset contains122,217 RPIswith a1:2 positive to negative ratio.

sections.title: RNA family-based train/test split
content: Dataset split intoTRinter(training) andTSfam(test) setsbased on RNA families.
Non-homologous dataguarantees evaluations on truly unseen interactions.
